rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, September 27, 2017 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant Fraser's Boiler Service, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is off calendar at the moving party's request. = (503/CML)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES CARTER ET AL VS. SOCO WEST, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel-Civil
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, September 27, 2017 in Department 503, Line 3. David F. Beach, Bishop Barry's motion to be relieved as counsel for defendant The McMichael Co.: hearing required. The hearing will be at 9:30a.m. Prior to the hearing, counsel must file and bring to the hearing a declaration in compliance with CRC 3.1362(c) (form MC-052). Counsel must bring a proposed order (form MC-053) to the hearing. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. = (503/CML)

rulings.Case Title: LESLIE STROTHERS VS. LAMONS GASKET COMPANY
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT / Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, September 27, 2017 in Department 503, Line 2. Defendant Temporary Plant Cleaners, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under either prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. While defendant may have demonstrated that Plant Maintenance employees did not expose plaintiff to asbestos-containing products or materials at the Tosco refinery in 1984, defendant did not demonstrate that such site was the only time plaintiff encountered Plant Maintenance employees. Plaintiff's deposition testimony and defendant's failure sufficiently question him on the topic preclude defendant from sustaining its initial burden. However, if defendant did sustain its initial burden, plaintiff's deposition testimony and declaration and the declaration of Charles Ay create a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to asbestos-containing insulation attributable to defendant. The Court must liberally construe plaintiffs' evidence and make all reasonable inferences in plaintiffs' favor. Plaintiff's objections to defendant's Exhibit V through Z are sustained. Defendant's objections to plaintiff's deposition testimony and declaration and Charles Ay's declaration are overruled. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/CML)
